message:
fix: require cluster selection in NodeDebug instead of silently defaulting (#3795) (#3814)

When no cluster was explicitly selected, NodeDebug fell back to the
'default' context, which in a multi-cluster environment could silently
run kubectl commands against the wrong cluster. Now the component
resolves the cluster from the selected node's metadata first, and if
no cluster can be determined, blocks execution with an error toast and
inline warning instead of defaulting.

Co-authored-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1900,7 +1900,8 @@
     "categoryKubernetes": "Kubernetes",
     "categoryNetwork": "Network",
     "categoryStorage": "Storage",
-    "commandCompleted": "Command completed"
+    "commandCompleted": "Command completed",
+    "clusterRequired": "Select a cluster before running commands — defaulting could target the wrong cluster"
   },
   "predictiveHealth": {
     "criticalCount": "{{count}} critical",
